goal: Remediate R1 findings for cxs session sources foundation

summary.title: Remediate cxs session-source R1 findings
summary.what: Fixed the R1 release blocker where source-aware read commands exposed raw SQLite schema errors against pre-source indexes, aligned README and the distributable cxs skill with checkout-vs-installed CLI boundaries, updated coverage design docs to include source as a selector dimension, and recorded the remediation in the workflow control plane.
summary.why: R1 found that the multi-source foundation was technically present but not release-safe: old local indexes failed with raw no-such-column errors, documentation over-described unreleased --source behavior for installed CLIs, and the coverage design still described single-source implication rules.
summary.user_goal: Remediate R1 findings for cxs session sources foundation

decisions.point: Old source-unaware indexes on read-only commands
decisions.chose: Add a source-aware read schema guard that returns index_schema_upgrade_required before query SQL runs.
decisions.rationale: find/list/read-range/read-page/stats must stay read-only, while sync remains the only command allowed to migrate or rebuild index state.
rejected: Let better-sqlite3 no such column errors reach users

decisions.point: Compatibility path for installed CLI and skill users
decisions.chose: Document how to detect older installed CLIs that do not support --source and tell agents to omit source flags or update the CLI.
decisions.rationale: E1 proved the npm/PATH/global skill layers can lag the checkout, so release-facing docs must not imply the installed command already has checkout-only behavior.
rejected: Pretend the current global cxs skill or npm package already includes the branch behavior

decisions.chose: Make source an explicit selector and coverage dimension, with implication false across sources.
decisions.rationale: The implementation already scopes selector implication by source; the design doc needed to match that current truth before closeout.
rejected: Leave source-aware coverage as an implementation-only detail

rejected.alternative: Implement or expose Claude Code adapter behavior during remediation
rejected.reason: The workflow goal explicitly reserves Claude Code for a future adapter and keeps this release public-source surface Codex-only.

review_notes: Verification passed: npm run test -- src/cli.test.ts, npm run check, default old-index checkout smoke for status/list/stats, and fresh E1 artifact smoke for list/find/read-page.

Mainline preflight overlap is expected against earlier proposed intents from the same stacked session-sources workflow; .ml-cache/mainline-signals/latest.md reported no matching guards, risks, or follow-ups.
No npm release, global CLI update, global skill update, push, PR, watcher, daemon, realtime sync, or public Claude Code adapter was performed.

architectural_claims: Read-only content commands now require the source-aware index schema before running SQL that references source identity columns.
status remains able to inspect old indexes without migrating them.
coverage implication is documented as source-scoped; cross-source implication is invalid.
behavioral_changes: find/list/read-range/read-page/stats return index_schema_upgrade_required instead of raw SQLite source_id column errors for old indexes.
README and skill guidance now distinguish source-aware checkout behavior from older installed CLI behavior.
Workflow recovery docs now mark all waves complete with R1 remediation evidence.

api_changes.signature: JSON error code index_schema_upgrade_required for find/read-range/read-page/list/stats

data_model_changes.name: source-aware index read requirement
data_model_changes.location: SQLite sessions and coverage schema checks
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Existing old indexes are upgraded by running cxs sync; read-only commands do not migrate.

migration_notes: Old source-unaware indexes need a sync command to add/backfill source identity and coverage columns before source-aware read commands can query them.
